jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Platform
jobs.fit: Platform framework ownership, TypeScript/Scala/Node.js, data contracts, distributed systems.

anthropic.profile: Staff-level Software Engineer with 17+ years building platform infrastructure and distributed backend systems. At Credit Karma, designed and own the Portal Provider framework relied on by 90%+ of portals clients. MS CS, ASU 2021.

snapshot.company_type: Private (venture-backed). Last valued ~$60B+ (2025). No IPO announced; high growth potential if AI market holds.
snapshot.salary_range: Senior SWE Bay Area: $450K-$650K TC typical; Staff/Lead $700K-$890K+ (Levels.fyi 2026).
snapshot.growth_note: Fastest-growing frontier AI lab after OpenAI. Equity upside if IPO or secondary liquidity events.

how_to_crack.style: Structured loop, high bar. Mix of hard coding, infra/system design, and values alignment.
how_to_crack.rounds: 5-6: recruiter screen -> 2x coding -> system design -> infra/platform design -> values/culture.
how_to_crack.coding: Heavy LeetCode (Hard). Also distributed-systems flavored problems. Less pure puzzle, more real-world constraints at Staff level.
prep_plan: 4-6 weeks LeetCode: NeetCode 150 + 30 Hard (graphs, DP, concurrency)
Prep 3 system designs: deployment pipeline, service mesh, observability platform
Prep 2 infra designs: service registry, multi-tenant auth at scale
Read Anthropic core views + Responsible Scaling Policy
Practice Finagle EOL + 100% uptime modernization story (5 min + deep dive)
Mock 1 values round: AI safety, long-term impact, intellectual humility

latest_questions.question: Describe a technical misjudgment that delayed a project - what did you learn?

Design inference batching on a single GPU maximizing utilization under synchronous user waits
Design distributed search for 1B documents at 1M QPS with sharding and LLM inference scaling
Design a batch inferencing API over GPU clusters (scheduling, cold start, model caching)
Implement LRU cache with serialization and evolving constraints
Build a multi-threaded web crawler with deduplication and rate limiting
Implement an in-memory database with SET/GET/DELETE, then add TTL, filtered scans, and file compression levels
